title: Eunox - CloudWatch Logs Insights queries (Sprint-1 OBS parity)
description: AWS multi-cloud parity for infra/sentinel/analytic-rules.json. These five queries detect the same Sprint-1 OBS patterns as the Sentinel rules: denial spikes, write attempts under read-only mode, invalid-token bursts, kill-switch activation, and token-revocation spikes. Run them either ad-hoc in the CloudWatch Logs Insights console or schedule them with `aws logs put-query-definition` / a CloudWatch Metric Filter + Alarm pair. The companion CloudFormation snippet in `cloudwatch-alarms.yaml` materializes alarm-driven incidents.

queries.name: eunox-deny-spike
queries.displayName: Capability denial spike from a single agent/session
queries.description: Five or more capability-token denials within five minutes from the same agent or session. Possible prompt-injection attempt or runaway agent loop.
queries.query:
fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /Action denied/ or @message like /AUTHORIZATION_FAILED/ or @message like /INVALID_TOKEN/
| parse @message /(?<payloadJson>\{.*\})\s*$/
| filter ispresent(payloadJson)
| fields jsonParse(payloadJson) as payload
| filter ispresent(payload.agentId) or ispresent(payload.sessionId) or ispresent(payload.metadata.sessionId)
| stats count() as denials by coalesce(payload.metadata.sessionId, payload.sessionId) as sessionId, payload.agentId as agentId, bin(5m)
| filter denials >= 5

queries.name: eunox-write-in-readonly
queries.displayName: Write attempt from a read-only session
queries.description: An agent session that was issued only read scopes attempted a write operation. Indicates either a buggy agent or an active jailbreak attempt.
queries.query:
fields @timestamp, @message
| parse @message /(?<payloadJson>\{.*\})\s*$/
| filter ispresent(payloadJson)
| fields jsonParse(payloadJson) as payload
| filter payload.logType = "audit"
| filter payload.decision = "deny"
| filter payload.action in ["write", "delete", "update", "create"]
| filter (payload.reason like /Insufficient permissions/) or (payload.reason like /Invalid audience/)
| filter ispresent(coalesce(payload.metadata.sessionId, payload.sessionId))
| display @timestamp, payload.agentId as agentId, payload.action as action, payload.reason as reason

queries.name: eunox-invalid-token-burst
queries.description: More than 20 invalid-signature or expired-token errors in a 5-minute window. Possible token-forgery attempt or a clock-skew incident in the fleet.
queries.query:
fields @timestamp, @message, @logStream
| filter @message like /INVALID_TOKEN/ or @message like /EXPIRED_TOKEN/ or @message like /invalid signature/
| stats count() as invalid_tokens by bin(5m), @logStream
| filter invalid_tokens > 20

queries.name: eunox-kill-switch-activated
queries.description: Operational event: an admin activated the global, session-scoped, or agent-scoped kill switch. Forwarded as a finding so the on-call channel always sees a record.
queries.query:
fields @timestamp, @message, @logStream
| filter @message like /Kill switch activated/ or @message like /KILL_ALL_AGENTS/ or @message like /kill switch enabled/

queries.name: eunox-token-revocation-spike
queries.description: More than 10 token revocations through the admin API within 5 minutes. Could indicate an automated incident-response action or an attacker abusing operator credentials.
queries.query:
fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /Token revoked via admin API/
| stats count() as revocations by bin(5m)
| filter revocations > 10
